mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly expansion in real GDP (GDPC1) reduces equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sharply, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of employment levels (PAYEMS declining) and rising unemployment (UNRATE increasing) signals a labor market recession that precedes equity drawdowns, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained environment, a decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) below 90 indicates capacity bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO drops below 90, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero during periods of high industrial capacity utilization.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances (RSAFS) reflect expanding household purchasing power and consumption demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with rising RSAFS are statistically indistinguishable from flat periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it triggers a recession warning that increases equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for equity returns in a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL above 3.5) and restrictive policy (FEDFUNDS above 4.0%) creates a stagflationary pressure that compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-occurring high-inflation/high-rate periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led downturn regime, a drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 800 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and consumer confidence, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 800, or the monthly housing starts data shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent equity returns over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
